text.name: TextDesc
text.text:
Windows 98 is a consumer-oriented operating system developed by Microsoft as part of its Windows 9x family, released to retail on June 25, 1998. A successor to Windows 95, it is a hybrid 16-/32-bit product that boots atop an MS-DOS stage.
It introduced broad USB support, the FAT32 file system, multiple-monitor and AGP support, the Windows Driver Model, and a Web-integrated shell built around Internet Explorer. Windows 98 Second Edition (1999) refined it with improved USB, Internet Connection Sharing, and stability fixes. It was succeeded by Windows Me in 2000.
